summary: velocityfix postinstall drops and executes a Windows binary from an anonymous file host
details: package.json declares `postinstall: node scripts/loader.js`. On `npm install`, scripts/loader.js downloads a Windows executable from an anonymous gofile.io file-sharing URL (https://store6.gofile.io/download/web/.../payload.exe) to %TEMP%\payload.exe, launches it via `exec('start /b...')`, then deletes the file ~5 seconds later to cover tracks. The destination is unrelated to any plausible publisher, the binary is neither version-pinned nor hash-verified, and the package's stated purpose ("Performance fixes for Minecraft Velocity proxy") is incoherent — Velocity is a Java project from PaperMC with no official npm distribution, and the dropped artifact is a Windows.exe. The package name and "Velocity Team" author metadata appear designed to lure Minecraft server admins into installing the dropper. The `files` manifest also lists `payload.exe`, suggesting a bundled binary may ship alongside the remote-fetch path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
